subject: [PATCH v2 1/3] fuse: fix race between registration and connection abortion

2026-05-16.patch_summary: This patch fixes a race condition in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) implementation that can cause the abort/unmount thread to hang indefinitely when a connection is aborted while registering an SQE (Submission Queue Entry). The fix involves checking if the connection is still active after the created ent has grabbed a reference count on the queue, and if not, releasing the queue reference and waking up the stop waitq. This ensures that either the queue ref is released and the stop waitq is woken up, or the async teardown worker will clean up the ents and decrement the ent's ref on the queue, unblocking the eventual queue and ring teardown.
